characters.description: A ruler opposing Asshai who awakens after a severe poisoning. He accepts Frisha and Tieran's relationship and remains intent on protecting his people despite the unidentified attack.
characters.name: Rezkin

characters.description: Rezkin's former intended partner, now openly involved with Tieran. She helps manage the crisis around Rezkin's poisoning and the appearance of two identical companions.
characters.name: Frisha

characters.description: Frisha’s partner, who intends to marry her if she agrees. Rezkin accepts their relationship without resentment and wants both of them protected.
characters.name: Tieran Nirius

characters.description: Rezkin's chosen king's mage, though still formally a journeyman. He seeks full standing and proposes entering Asshai to persuade former mage allies to abandon King Caydean.
characters.name: Wesson

characters.description: Rezkin's captive friend and trainee. His location and condition are unknown, and Rezkin is determined to retrieve him personally despite having only just recovered from poisoning.
characters.name: Tamarin Blackwater

characters.description: Tam's fellow quarry slave, bound to him by an enchanted neck-chain. He survives attacks beside Tam and questions whether the quarry stone truly acts under Tam's claimed protection.
characters.name: Uthe

characters.description: Rezkin's trusted striker and security leader. He is already known as a close protector before this volume begins.
characters.name: Kai

characters.description: Rezkin's former trainer and a wary protector who keeps watch throughout the poisoning. He identifies himself as a striker even though Rezkin expects that he may eventually try to kill him.
characters.name: Farson

characters.description: A healer allied with Rezkin who prepares to join the expedition seeking Tam. Her skills make her part of the small party assembled for the rescue.
characters.name: Reaylin de Voss

characters.description: Reaylin's father and the leader of the Asshaian rebels near Benbrick. With Caydean's army due within a day, he asks Wesson to unite the local mages behind the defense.
characters.name: Peyton DeVos

characters.description: The missing prince of Asshai, whose absence is part of the unresolved succession conflict at the start of the volume.
characters.name: Prince Thresson

characters.description: The king of Asshai and an established threat to Rezkin's people. Opposition to his rule shapes the conflict already under way when this volume begins.
characters.name: King Caydean

characters.description: Wesson's former master. He publicly supports Wesson's judgment and ability when the gathered mages of Benbrick must choose their leader before the royal attack.
characters.name: Equestrius

characters.description: A journeyman and natural battle mage among the gathered mages at Benbrick. She joins Wesson with most of the local faction, although her brother remains with the opposing royal force.
characters.name: Alantra Doon

characters.description: A battle mage with an opposing royal force. Alantra Doon’s brother remains aligned with him.
characters.name: Battle Master Rhone

characters.description: An Asshaian operative reported to be directing the siege of Gage. His force seeks a weapon inside the city while mages prepare marked vessels under his control.
characters.name: the Sen

characters.description: The local authority assigned to observe Rezkin while a hidden council assesses whether his power makes him dangerous. He becomes Rezkin's custodian and explains the history of human-born spiritua.
characters.name: the spirit leader

characters.description: A hidden people’s military commander who regards Rezkin with suspicion. She believes his lack of strength could postpone the madness expected of him.
characters.name: the spirit general

characters.description: The wolf-shifting daughter of the leopard leaders. Rezkin saves her from lethal venom, accidentally creating a bond that he alters so she can live independently and regard him as a friend.
characters.name: the bonded wolf-shifter

characters.description: An ancient pointed-eared person who permanently chose a wolf's form. He agrees to oppose the demons and directs Rezkin's party toward the lion tribe and the missing elder they seek.
characters.name: the great wolf

characters.description: An ancient pointed-eared figure leading the lion tribe. He admits murdering lion children born in other forms and blaming rival tribes to manufacture a war, after which his own people take him into custody.
characters.name: the captive lion leader
